content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Find another integer X(X>N)X (X > N)X(X>N) such that the following conditions are satisfied :


XXX is not a multiple of NNN.
The value  ∣gcd(N,X)−lcm(N,X)∣|gcd(N,X)-lcm(N,X)|∣gcd(N,X)−lcm(N,X)∣ is as small as possible.
If there are multiple answers for XXX, print any.

For reference, gcd(u,v)gcd(u, v)gcd(u,v) is the greatest common divisor between uuu and vvv, while lcm(u,v)lcm(u, v)lcm(u,v) refers to the least common multiple.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.


Output Format
For each testcase, print any suitable XXX satisfying the above two conditions for the given NNN.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5*10^12
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer X on its own line, satisfying the conditions described in the statement.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=3 (prime) the smallest divisor is 3, so X = 3 + 3/3 = 4. For N=4 the smallest divisor is 2, so X = 4 + 4/2 = 6.
N=2 (prime) → X=2+1=3. N=15 smallest divisor 3 → X=15+5=20. N=9973 is prime → X=9973+1=9974.

solution.approach: For a given N we write N = g * a where a >= 2 is a divisor of N and g = N / a. Let X = g * b with b > a and gcd(a, b) = 1. The expression to minimise is |gcd(N, X) - lcm(N, X)| = g * (a * b - 1). The smallest possible b is a + 1 (always coprime with a). Substituting b = a + 1 gives X = g * (a + 1) = N + N / a. To minimise the value we need the smallest possible a, i.e., the smallest divisor of N greater than 1 (the smallest prime factor). If N is prime, the only divisor >1 is N itself, leading to X = N + 1. Therefore the algorithm is: find the smallest divisor d of N (d = N if N is prime) and output X = N + N / d. Finding the smallest divisor can be done by trial division up to sqrt(N), which is fast enough for N ≤ 10^12.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * sqrt(N)) – trial division up to sqrt(N) for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
